item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I was at a novote when this first went on the mainlist. At the time, I must have thought this was missing something. Now, I've mostly come around after rereading it, and I do (mostly) enjoy this article. But, thinking back — yeah, there are some things amiss here:

When SCP-3784 is within an unbroken line of sight of at least one human in REM sleep, SCP-3784 will kindle itself if not already lit, and initiate an INCUBUS event.

Based upon both the shadows cast by SCP-3784 and post-INCUBUS event interviews, a convocation of both humanoid and non-humanoid entities will typically surround the subject and begin to enact some kind of farcical debate with each other.

There are many such sentences in this piece with awkward wording and tone. My very first instinct is drawing the obvious connection between this page and typical Series I "magic item" pages. And this page really does capture that Series I feel — right down to the (and I'm sorry to say) awkward, non-concise passages like those listed above. On a longer piece I might ignore them, but, since this is such a short article, it really had the opportunity to nail its description, and it didn't.

Granted, this was written during the Jam Contest, so I think it's worth being charitable toward it. And this does have an undeniable charm and horror to it.

So, yeah, while I think this could be improved, I did enjoy it.

item.title: Solid
item.content:encoded: I liked this story a good bit. I'm not going to lie, the premise of a lamp that causes nightmares would sound pretty bad if it was just pitched to me, but your execution was great. I really would have liked if there were logs about one of the debates or even an interview trying to talk with one of the Class-Ds after going under. Still, I definitely liked the story and the last tie-in!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh-Oh, ya friggin moron! Your post slot got YOINKED! Tag your friends to YOINK! their author posts!

But yeah, DrChandra continues to give me happiness by writing about my guy 4116. Hopefully I'll be able to contribute too, once I finally get around to posting stuff. If I knew enough about MiCkey-D's lore, I'd return the favor.
